text.title: Dependency Spike Kill-Criteria Template Worked Example
text.summary: One worked end-to-end example showing a Dependency Spike Kill-Criteria Template artefact filled from a realistic input bundle. Trace runs from input brief → evidence gathering → schema fill → validator pass → review surfacing.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: Solo dev evaluates `bun` as a Node.js replacement for a Next.js side-project. Pre-spike criteria: cold-start <300ms, bundle-build <2s, no AGPL deps, ≥1 maintainer release in last 90d, dx friction <3 incidents in 1-day trial.

list.item: Step 1 — prerequisites_ok=true; all input refs reachable.
Step 2 — evidence array populated with ≥1 citation per non-trivial field.
Step 4 — validator script exits 0; no violations.

section.title: Outcome
section.p: The artefact is auditable by a downstream reviewer without re-deriving the reasoning. Every non-trivial field traces back to an evidence row; rejected fields surface as structured skip-with-reason instead of polluting the library with degraded output.
